node_2: limitations of existing color style transfer methods
edge: Neural Preset technique is introduced to address the limitations of existing color style transfer methods, including visual artifacts, vast memory requirement, and slow style switching speed.

Neural Preset technique is based on Deterministic Neural Color Mapping (DNCM) to consistently operate on each pixel via an image-adaptive color mapping matrix, avoiding artifacts and supporting high-resolution inputs with a small memory footprint.

Neural Preset technique has a two-stage pipeline dividing the task into color normalization and stylization, allowing efficient style switching by extracting color styles as presets and reusing them on normalized input images.
Neural Preset technique describes training via a self-supervised strategy due to the unavailability of pairwise datasets.
Neural Preset technique demonstrates various advantages over existing methods through comprehensive evaluations.

Neural Preset technique shows that the trained model can naturally support multiple applications without fine-tuning, including low-light image enhancement, underwater image correction, image dehazing, and image harmonization.
Color retouching becomes an indispensable operation before sharing photos in public to help express the story captured on social media platforms like Instagram and Facebook.
Color retouching is an operation that enhances the visual appearance of images, while image filters are presets provided by photo editing tools to alter the colors in images.
Image filters and Look-Up Tables (LUTs) are both color style presets provided by photo editing tools to assist users in exploring different color styles efficiently.
Color style transfer techniques aim to automatically transfer the color style from one retouched image to another image, enhancing the visual appeal of the latter.
Color style transfer techniques utilize a well-retouched image as the style image to map the color style to another input image, ensuring consistent color styles across diverse images.

Earlier color style transfer methods focus on retouching images based on low-level feature statistics from the style image, which sometimes results in unexpected color changes due to neglecting high-level information.
Deep learning based models for color style transfer often suffer from producing unrealistic artifacts in the stylized image due to color mapping based on convolutional models.
Convolutional models perform color mapping based on an image patch, which may lead to inconsistent outputs for pixels with the same value
Convolutional models still fail to prevent artifacts robustly despite the proposal of auxiliary constraints and post-processing strategies
Convolutional models cannot handle high-resolution images like 8K due to their huge runtime memory footprint
Even using a GPU with 24GB of memory, most recent models suffer from the out-of-memory problem when processing 4K images
Color style transfer carried out as a single-stage process in most models requires running the whole model every time, making it inefficient in switching styles
Neural Preset technique is presented with two core designs to overcome the limitations related to convolutional models and high-resolution images
Neural Preset leverages Deterministic Neural Color Mapping (DNCM) as an alternative to the color mapping process based on convolutional models for more effective color conversion
DNCM converts pixels of the same color to a specific color, avoiding unrealistic artifacts effectively
DNCM operates on each pixel independently with a small memory footprint, supporting very high-resolution inputs
Unlike adaptive 3D LUTs or automatic filters, DNCM can model arbitrary color mapping
DNCM can model arbitrary color mappings with only a few hundred learnable parameters.
Neural Preset carries out color style transfer in two stages to enable fast style switching.
The first stage builds a nDNCM from the input image for color normalization.
The second stage builds a sDNCM from the style image for color stylization.
The parameters of sDNCM can be stored as color style presets and reused by different input images.
Since there are no pairwise datasets available, we propose a new self-supervised strategy for Neural Preset to be trainable.
Our comprehensive evaluations demonstrate that Neural Preset outperforms state-of-the-art methods significantly in various aspects.
Neural Preset can produce faithful results for 8K images and can provide consistent color style transfer results across video frames without post-processing.
Neural Preset achieves ∼28× speedup on a Nvidia RTX3090 GPU, supporting real-time performances at 4K resolution.
The Nvidia RTX3090 GPU provides a 28x speedup, enabling real-time performances at 4K resolution.

The trained model can be applied to various color mapping tasks without the need for fine-tuning, such as low-light image enhancement, underwater image correction, image dehazing, and image harmonization.
Color Style Transfer differs from artistic style transfer, as it focuses on shifting colors only between images instead of altering both textures and colors.
Traditional methods typically match the statistics of low-level features, such as mean and variance of images or histograms of filter responses.
Many methods that utilize convolutional neural networks have been proposed for color style transfer to improve the process.
Yoo et al. introduced a model featuring wavelet pooling/unpooling to minimize distortions during the color style transfer process.
An et al. utilized network architecture search to explore a more effective asymmetric model for color style transfer.

Filters and LUTs perform deterministic color mapping to produce consistent outputs for the same input pixel values.
Filter-based methods integrate a finite number of image filters for basic color adjustments such as brightness and contrast.
LUT-based methods merge several template LUTs using predicted coefficients due to the large number of learnable parameters.
Recent image enhancement methods have used filters/LUTs combined with CNNs for color mapping with some drawbacks.
Combining filters/LUTs with CNNs for color mapping has clear drawbacks according to the mentioned methods.

The affine bilateral grid may model complex color mapping with fewer learnable parameters but cannot provide deterministic color mapping, potentially leading to inharmonious colors in different image regions.
DNCM is proposed as an alternative to the aforementioned schemes for color style transfer.

DNCM is proposed instead of adopting other schemes, and it is used for color mapping which is related to SSL as SSL has been widely explored for pre-training and some works solve vision tasks via SSL.

nDNCM and sDNCM are proposed to be used together in the context to achieve the tasks of converting input images to a space containing 'image content' and transferring 'image content' to target color style, respectively.

The concept of 'image content' is proposed to be represented in a normalized color style space by learning through back-propagation, where images with the same content but different color styles should have a consistent appearance.

The encoder E is modified to output parameters d and r, which are used in nDNCM and sDNCM, respectively, for converting input images to 'image content' space and transferring 'image content' to target color style.

The color style of a style image Is is transferred to an input image Ic through the stages of converting Ic to Zc in a normalized color space via nDNCM and then transferring Zc to a stylized image Ys via sDNCM.
nDNCM and sDNCM are associated with different projection matrices P and Q, respectively, indicating separate functionalities in the context.
